target.name: Sofar Ocean
target.founder: Tim Janssen, Brad Lassey, Alex Wight

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $75M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Sofar Ocean is a Series B-stage company operating in the ocean and maritime technology sector. The company has raised $75M+ to date across its funding rounds. Valuation analysis is constrained by the absence of public comparables in this space and limited private peer transaction data suitable for direct benchmarking.

Given data limitations, stage-progression methodology provides the primary valuation signal. Using total capital raised ($75M) as a base proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to subsequent rounds yields an implied valuation of $142M. This reflects the capital intensity and developmental stage of frontier ocean technology ventures. Sensitivity analysis around this multiplier—testing 1.6x and 2.2x bounds—produces a valuation range of $121M (low) to $164M (high).

The single-method triangulation yields a median point estimate of $142M, supported by stage-appropriate step-up calibration but constrained by the absence of direct-peer revenue, EBITDA, or transaction multiples for validation. Key uncertainties include actual revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and path-to-margin assumptions—none of which are observable in available data. Any valuation refinement would require operational metrics and comparable transaction evidence from the ocean-tech subsector.
